subtask: Review README.md for metaswarm documentation completeness

key_findings: Metaswarm section exists as inline bold paragraph only — no standalone ## heading
Core 50x5=250 worker architecture correctly documented in multiple places
One usage example provided: stampede.sh --metaswarm --run-id ... --count 3
swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl mentioned as runtime proof
Missing: decision guidance on when to use metaswarm vs normal mode
Missing: metaswarm-specific task-file JSON schema
Missing: partial/failure output shape beyond one sentence
No step-by-step explanation of how launcher switches to commander mode internally
No architecture diagram for metaswarm/commander topology
--count meaning changes (workers in normal mode, commanders in metaswarm) — undocumented
child-agents.jsonl and swarm-state.json file schemas completely absent
Depth Guard concept not explained in README
No documentation of collaboration bus at .stampede/{run_id}/collab/
No failure mode documentation (partial bundle, circuit breaker, silent downgrade prevention)
Commander vs worker agent distinction not explained for end users
No list of premium vs standard models in README
Only --model flag documented; --models (multi-model) flag not mentioned
No per-agent model selection guidance
Metaswarm mentions uses premium child-model overrides by default without naming models
General note about lightweight vs powerful models exists (line 377) but no formal policy
Commander vs worker model assignment not documented
No collaboration bus concept documented anywhere in README
Architecture is intentionally isolation-first: agents explicitly share nothing
No Convergence Broadcasts or cross-agent signaling documented
No context/discovery sharing mechanism described
Commander-worker communication is one-way telemetry only (children -> commander -> monitor)
Collaboration bus at .stampede/{run_id}/collab/ (referenced in agent files) absent from README
This is partly architectural: system designed as embarrassingly parallel, not collaborative
SwarmMesh: ZERO mentions anywhere in the entire repository
swarm-mesh and swarm_mesh: ZERO matches in any file
No mesh topology documentation exists
No SwarmMesh scaffold or template files exist
Architecture uses centralized filesystem queue hub-model, not peer-to-peer mesh
Closest related feature is metaswarm/Swarm Command SS-250 (commander hierarchy)
SwarmMesh appears to be a gap: feature not yet built and not documented

synthesis.overall_assessment: The README covers the basics of metaswarm mode but lacks depth for operators who need to debug, extend, or understand the full architecture.

documentation_gaps_found.detail: Metaswarm is described inline under Run section only. A dedicated ## Metaswarm Mode section would improve discoverability.
The --models flag for multi-model configurations is completely absent. Only --model (single) is shown.

documentation_gaps_found.gap: No premium model list or policy
documentation_gaps_found.detail: Premium models are referenced (premium child-model overrides) but never listed or defined. Users cannot know which models qualify.

documentation_gaps_found.gap: No file format schemas for swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl
documentation_gaps_found.detail: These files are mentioned as runtime proof but zero schema/format documentation exists.
Depth Guard concept (preventing recursive spawning beyond depth 2) is referenced in agent files but never explained in README.
.stampede/{run_id}/collab/ collaboration bus referenced in agent file but absent from README. Likely by architectural design (isolation-first) but should be noted.
SwarmMesh concept is completely absent from the codebase. Not a documentation gap but an unbuilt feature gap.

documentation_gaps_found.gap: No architecture diagram for metaswarm topology
documentation_gaps_found.detail: The ASCII architecture diagram covers standard worker flow only. Metaswarm commander hierarchy needs its own diagram.

documentation_gaps_found.gap: No failure mode documentation for commanders
documentation_gaps_found.detail: No documentation on what happens when a commander fails, what partial means, or circuit breaker behavior.
In normal mode --count = number of workers. In metaswarm mode --count = number of commanders. This semantic change is never explained.

well_documented_areas: 50x5=250 worker architecture per commander clearly stated
Filesystem IPC design and atomic operations explained well
Basic --metaswarm flag and single usage example present
Commander agent purpose and location documented in AGENTS.md
priority_recommendations: Add dedicated ## Metaswarm Mode section with architecture diagram
Add file format schemas for swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl
Document failure modes and partial result handling
Clarify --count semantic difference between normal and metaswarm modes
Add premium model list and selection policy
